mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trajectory in the federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of observations despite consecutive FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower when UNRATE accelerates by >0.4pp compared to stable unemployment periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and rising consumer price inflation indicate demand-pull inflation eroding real purchasing power, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the RSAFS-CPIAUCSL divergence and SPX 5-day returns exceeds 0.05 over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy business investment, which supports corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of INDPRO uptrend observations during early-cycle recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when HOUST drops below 1100.0 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market that sustains wage-driven consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 50% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly boosts corporate earnings per share, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth accelerates sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, subsequent federal funds rate hikes trigger aggressive valuation repricing in growth-sensitive equities, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS increases in the same month.
